text.tspan: END-TO-END / UI TESTS (few, slow, brittle)
spin up the WHOLE SYSTEM u2014 all services + their DBs + external mocks
u2022 minutes per run, full of timing flakes
u2022 hard to debug failures (which service broke?)
u2022 hard to keep stable across many teams
USE SPARINGLY u2014 only critical happy-path business flows
CONTRACT TESTS (one per consumeru2194producer pair)
verify that 'I send X, I get Y back' agreement holds
u2022 consumer-driven: consumer publishes its expectations
u2022 producer runs them as part of CI
u2022 fast u2014 no integrated environment needed
u2022 catches breaking API changes BEFORE merge
THE KEY MICROSERVICES TEST
SERVICE COMPONENT TESTS (one set per service)
spin up THE SERVICE PROCESS in isolation
stub or in-memory all its dependencies
u2022 black-box: test the service via its real API
u2022 white-box: full code path inside
u2022 fast u2014 seconds per test
u2022 catches integration bugs (DB, framework, serialization)
INTEGRATION TESTS (per side adapter)
test service u2194 real DB, service u2194 real Kafka, service u2194 real cache
u2022 verifies adapters work against real dependencies
u2022 docker-compose / testcontainers is the standard tool
UNIT TESTS (many, fast, focused)
pure functions, domain logic, no I/O
u2022 milliseconds per test, thousands per service
u2022 high coverage, fast feedback loop
